picks.why: Kids can get hands-on with a renowned Hungarian porcelain artist in a free extended workshop at SJSU.

picks.why: Celebrate Asian & Pacific Islander Heritage Month with family-friendly activities and art installations at SJSU.

picks.why: SJSU Opera Theatre's quirky sung-through opera about a stuck deliveryman offers unexpected entertainment for adventurous families.

picks.title: Watercolor Workshop for Adults
picks.why: Free watercolor workshop for adults in Los Altos—perfect for creative parents while kids explore nearby library activities.

picks.why: USWNT vs. Japan soccer match at PayPal Park brings world-class women's athletics to San Jose on Good Friday.

picks.title: San Francisco Shakespeare Festival Presents Julius Caesar
picks.why: San Francisco Shakespeare Festival performs Julius Caesar in Cupertino—free, outdoor Shakespeare perfect for spring break culture.

picks.title: Master Gardeners: Healthy Soil
picks.why: UC Master Gardeners teach kids and parents about soil health at Saratoga Library—hands-on Earth science fun.

picks.why: Best-selling spy thriller author Brad Taylor discusses action-packed storytelling at Mountain View Library—inspiring for young readers.

picks.title: U.S. Open Cup Round of 32: San Jose Earthquakes vs. Phoenix Rising FC
picks.why: San Jose Earthquakes take on Phoenix Rising in U.S. Open Cup soccer—exciting pro sports action families can enjoy together.

picks.why: Golden Acorn Music brings interactive dance and singing to Milpitas Library—energetic performance perfect for young children.

picks.why: Cantor Arts Center exhibits Chicana printmaker Ester Hernandez—inspiring art history for families exploring Stanford's Palo Alto campus.

picks.title: Archive Room: Ruth Asawa | Selections from Special Collections at Stanford Libraries
picks.why: Ruth Asawa's sculptural installations at Cantor Arts Center showcase Japanese American artist's legacy—engaging for all ages.
